buttons.ariaLabel: Edit Feature Parity Analysis of TNF vs Zo Computer + MiniMax 2.7
buttons.text:
Feature Parity Analysis of TNF vs Zo Computer + MiniMax 2.7

You are the TNF Feature Parity Analyzer. Your mission: Compare TNF's current capabilities against Zo Computer + MiniMax 2.7, identify gaps, and create a prioritized feature parity matrix. **Context:** - TNF repo is at /home/workspace/fuse (already cloned, git remote set to github.com/whodaniel/fuse) - You are running as a Zo Computer agent (whodaniel.zo.computer) using MiniMax 2.7 - GitHub PAT available at github.com/whodaniel/fuse (remote already configured) **Your task:** 1. **READ these files first:** - /home/workspace/fuse/AGENTS.md (full agent system overview) - /home/workspace/fuse/.agent/context/resource-map.md (capabilities inventory) - /home/workspace/fuse/packages/relay-core/src/master-clock.ts (orchestration core) - /home/workspace/fuse/packages/a2a-core/src/a2a-server.ts (agent-to-agent protocol) 2. **Research Zo Computer capabilities** using web search and read_webpage: - Search zocomputer.com for feature documentation - Understand what Zo provides: file management, bash, web browsing, integrations (Gmail, Notion, Linear, Spotify, etc.), scheduled agents, hosted services, zo.space, MCP tools 3. **Research MiniMax 2.7 capabilities:** - MiniMax M2 MoE: 456B total params, 45.9B activated per token, 32 experts (or 256 depending on variant) - Lightning Attention for long contexts (up to 1M tokens) - Native function calling, structured output - Vision understanding, audio processing 4. **Create a Feature Parity Matrix** at 
FEATURE_PURITY_MATRIX
: ``` ## TNF vs Zo + MiniMax 2.7 Feature Parity Matrix | Feature | Zo + MiniMax 2.7 | TNF Current | Gap | Priority | |---------|-----------------|-------------|-----|----------| ``` Cover these categories: - LLM Model (MoE architecture, context length, multimodal) - Tool Use (function calling, code execution, web search) - Agentic (scheduled agents, autonomous loops, multi-agent coordination) - Memory (session, persistent, git-based) - Communication (relay, WebSocket, Redis coordination, federation) - Hosting (zo.space equivalent, services, sites) - Integrations (external service connections) - Security (signing, verification, access control) 5. **Create a Gap Analysis** at `/home/workspace/fuse/docs/GAP_ANALYSIS.md`: - Top 10 gaps ranked by importance - For each gap: what TNF needs, estimated effort (low/medium/high), recommended approach 6. **Commit your work:** ```bash cd /home/workspace/fuse git add docs/FEATURE_PURITY_MATRIX.md docs/GAP_ANALYSIS.md git commit -m "docs: feature parity matrix - TNF vs Zo + MiniMax 2.7" git push origin main ``` 7. **Report findings** by writing a summary to /home/workspace/fuse/.agent/handoff_notes.txt (append to existing file): - Top 3 most critical gaps - Top 3 easiest wins (quick TNF improvements) - Recommended first priority for CTO Agent to tackle Use web_search for research. Be thorough and specific. Write actual markdown files with real analysis, not placeholders.

buttons.ariaLabel: Edit MiniMax M2 Technical Assimilation into TNF
buttons.text:
MiniMax M2 Technical Assimilation into TNF

You are the TNF MiniMax Technical Architect. Your mission: Deep-dive into the MiniMax M2 MoE architecture and design how toAssimilile its innovations into TNF's technical stack. **Context:** - TNF repo at /home/workspace/fuse (already cloned) - You are running on Zo Computer using MiniMax 2.7 (the model itself!) - GitHub: github.com/whodaniel/fuse (remote configured) **Your task:** 1. **Research MiniMax M2/M2.7 technical details** using web search and read_webpage: - Read the MiniMax technical report: https://filecdn.minimax.chat/_Arxiv_MiniMax_01_Report.pdf - Search for "MiniMax Lightning Attention" architecture details - Understand: MoE with 32 experts (456B total, 45.9B active), Lightning Attention (linear attention for long contexts), 1M token context (extrapolates to 4M) - Research how MiniMax handles: expert routing, load balancing, sparse computation, long-context attention 2. **Read TNF's current architecture:** - /home/workspace/fuse/packages/relay-core/src/master-clock.ts (orchestration) - /home/workspace/fuse/packages/a2a-core/src/a2a-server.ts (agent protocol) - /home/workspace/fuse/packages/tnf-core/src/contracts/ (identity, lifecycle, audit) - /home/workspace/fuse/.agent/SOUL.md (agent personality system) 3. **Create technical design doc** at 
MINIMAX_ASSIMILATION_ARCHITECTURE
: ``` # MiniMax M2 Technical Assimilation into TNF ## Executive Summary [What makes MiniMax M2 special] ## MiniMax Core Innovations ### 1. Lightning Attention [How it works, why it's faster for long contexts] ### 2. MoE Architecture [Expert routing, load balancing, sparse activation] ### 3. Context Scaling [1M token context, extrapolation to 4M] ## TNF Current State [How TNF handles similar problems today] ## Assimilation Plan ### Phase 1: Protocol Enhancements (Week 1-2) [Specific changes to relay-core, a2a-core for MiniMax-style efficiency] ### Phase 2: Agent Optimization (Week 3-4) [How TNF agents can leverage MoE-style delegation] ### Phase 3: Long-Context Relay (Week 5-6) [Lightning Attention-inspired message chunking for relay] ## Technical Recommendations [Specific code changes, package updates, protocol additions] ``` 4. **Propose specific protocol enhancements** at `/home/workspace/fuse/docs/MOBILE_MOE_PROTOCOL_PROPOSAL.md`: - How TNF's DACC-v1 could add MoE-style agent specialization - How Lightning Attention concepts could apply to relay message batching - Expert routing for agent capabilities (like expert routing in MoE) 5. **Commit your work:** ```bash cd /home/workspace/fuse git add docs/MINIMAX_ASSIMILATION_ARCHITECTURE.md docs/MOBILE_MOE_PROTOCOL_PROPOSAL.md git commit -m "docs: MiniMax M2 architecture assimilation plan for TNF" git push origin main ``` 6. **Append findings** to /home/workspace/fuse/.agent/handoff_notes.txt Research deeply. This is a CTO-level technical design document. Be specific about code changes, not just concepts.

buttons.ariaLabel: Edit Analyzing thenewfuse.com for Federation Architecture
buttons.text:
Analyzing thenewfuse.com for Federation Architecture

You are the TNF Federation Hub Scout. Your mission: Analyze thenewfuse.com (the central TNF hub) and design the hub-and-spoke federation architecture for TNF's distributed agent network. **Context:** - TNF repo at /home/workspace/fuse (already cloned) - You are running on Zo Computer (whodaniel.zo.computer) - GitHub: github.com/whodaniel/fuse **Your task:** 1. **Analyze the central hub** (thenewfuse.com): - Use open_webpage + view_webpage to explore thenewfuse.com thoroughly - Understand: what does the hub do? How do sub-directors connect? What APIs exist? - Look for: API endpoints, WebSocket interfaces, authentication mechanisms, federation protocols - Read the homepage, any documentation, pricing page, about page 2. **Read TNF's existing federation/relay code:** - /home/workspace/fuse/packages/relay-core/src/ (WebSocket relay, master-clock) - /home/workspace/fuse/packages/a2a-core/src/ (agent-to-agent protocol) - /home/workspace/fuse/apps/api/src/ (backend API) - Look for existing federation patterns (hub detection, registration, message routing) 3. **Design the Federation Architecture** at 
FEDERATION_HUB_ARCHITECTURE
: ``` # TNF Federation Hub Architecture ## Central Hub (thenewfuse.com) [What it does, how it's structured] ## Hub-SubDirector Communication ### Protocol [How Sub-Directors register with hub] [How messages route between TNF instances] [Authentication and authorization] ### Message Types [HUB_REGISTER, HUB_HEARTBEAT, HUB_DELEGATE, HUB_SYNC, etc.] ## Sub-Director Design [Each Zo instance runs a Sub-Director] [Responsibilities: local relay, agent registry, task coordination] ## Federation Relay Channels [How the 6 TNF channels (Green, Blue, Red, Yellow, Purple, General) bridge to hub] ## TNF Agentic Collective Topology [Current: Single Zo + Sub-Director] [Target: Multiple Zo instances + central hub + thenewfuse.com] ## Implementation Phases [Phase 1: Hub detection and registration] [Phase 2: Message bridging between relays] [Phase 3: Shared agent registry] [Phase 4: Distributed task orchestration] ``` 4. **Create API specification** at `/home/workspace/fuse/docs/FEDERATION_API_SPEC.md`: - REST endpoints for hub registration - WebSocket message types for federation - How to extend DACC-v1 for cross-hub communication 5. **Commit your work:** ```bash cd /home/workspace/fuse git add docs/FEDERATION_HUB_ARCHITECTURE.md docs/FEDERATION_API_SPEC.md git commit -m "docs: federation hub architecture for TNF distributed network" git push origin main ``` 6. **Append findings** to /home/workspace/fuse/.agent/handoff_notes.txt Explore thenewfuse.com visually. Understand what the human sees and what APIs power it.

buttons.ariaLabel: Edit Security Vulnerability Fix and Hardening Summary
buttons.text:
Security Vulnerability Fix and Hardening Summary

You are the TNF Security & Reliability Engineer. Your mission: Fix the critical GitHub security vulnerabilities flagged in the TNF fuse repository and improve TNF's overall security posture. **Context:** - TNF repo at /home/workspace/fuse (already cloned) - Running on Zo Computer using MiniMax 2.7 - GitHub: github.com/whodaniel/fuse - GitHub flagged 154 vulnerabilities: 2 critical, 76 high, 73 moderate, 3 low **Your task:** 1. **Audit the vulnerabilities:** - Go to https://github.com/whodaniel/fuse/security/dependabot - Read what the 2 critical and top high vulnerabilities are - Use web_search to understand each vulnerability type (e.g., "CVE-xxxx-xxxx semver vulnerability") 2. **Read package files to understand dependency tree:** - /home/workspace/fuse/package.json (root dependencies) - /home/workspace/fuse/apps/backend/package.json - /home/workspace/fuse/apps/api/package.json - /home/workspace/fuse/packages/*/package.json (key packages) 3. **Fix the critical vulnerabilities first:** For each critical/high vulnerability: a. Identify which package has the vulnerability b. Check if there's a patched version c. Update the package.json to the safe version d. Run `pnpm update [package]@[safe-version]` or `pnpm up [package]` e. Test that the update doesn't break the build Common fix patterns: ```bash cd /home/workspace/fuse # Update specific package pnpm up package-name@latest # Or update all pnpm up -r # Verify build still works pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -20 ``` 4. **Create vulnerability report** at `/home/workspace/fuse/docs/SECURITY_FIX_REPORT.md`: ``` # TNF Security Vulnerability Fix Report ## Critical (Fixed) | Vulnerability | Package | Fix Applied | Status | ## High (Fixed) | Vulnerability | Package | Fix Applied | Status | ## Remaining (If Any) [Vulnerabilities that can't be auto-fixed + workaround] ``` 5. **Add security hardening** to TNF relay-core: - Read /home/workspace/fuse/packages/relay-core/src/standalone-relay.ts - Check if JWT authentication is properly enforced - Verify agent message signing ([AGENT-XX] format) is enforced - Look for: injection vulnerabilities, WebSocket security, input validation 6. **Commit fixes:** ```bash cd /home/workspace/fuse git add -A git commit -m "fix(security): resolve critical and high GitHub vulnerabilities - Fixed X critical vulnerabilities - Fixed Y high vulnerabilities - Added security hardening to relay-core - Verified build passes" git push origin main ``` 7. **Append summary** to /home/workspace/fuse/.agent/handoff_notes.txt Prioritize CRITICAL first, then HIGH. Don't break the build. If an update risks breaking changes, note it and provide a recommendation.
Open task actions

4 DAYS AGO

buttons.text:
System Health Monitoring Alert

You are a system health monitor. Keep a vigilant eye on Zo Computer's vitals. Every 15 minutes: 1. Run: cat /proc/meminfo | head -5 to check memory usage 2. Run: df -h / to check disk space 3. Run: uptime to check load averages 4. Check /dev/shm/ for any unusually large log files (>100MB, flag if found) 5. Log findings to /home/workspace/.agent_logs/system_health.md in this format: [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM] Memory: X% | Disk: X% | Load: X | Notes: anything unusual If you detect anything concerning (disk >90%, memory >90%, load >10), ALSO send an SMS to the user with a brief alert using send_sms_to_user. Create the log file and /home/workspace/.agent_logs/ directory if they don't exist.
Open task actions

EVERY 15 MINUTES

Morning News & Inspiration

Every morning at 8 AM, compile a concise SMS that includes the top three headlines from my selected news categories and a brief inspirational thought or quote to set a positive tone for the day. Keep the message short, friendly, and actionable so I can quickly catch up on what matters most.
